## 9. Friend of the Court (FOC) — Responsibilities & Accountability

The **Friend of the Court (FOC)** is created by statute in each judicial circuit under the Friend of the Court Act (PA 294 of 1982, MCL 552.501–552.535). The FOC is **not** a court — it is an office of the circuit court that assists with domestic relations cases.

> *Authority:* MCL 552.501 et seq. (Friend of the Court Act, PA 294 of 1982)

### FOC Referee Hearings & De Novo Review

FOC referees are **not judges**. They issue **recommended orders** that are subject to **de novo review** by the circuit court judge.
